description: Complete DALP platform screenshot reference, all 160 screens with explanations, grouped by section.

slides.title: Platform Access and Dashboard
slides.subtitle: Secure entry, role-based navigation, live command centre.

slides.title: Secure Authentication
slides.body: DALP supports passwordless passkey login alongside email/password credentials. Access is role-gated from the first click, ensuring only authorised participants reach the platform.

slides.title: Dashboard Overview
slides.body: The main dashboard gives operators a live command centre: portfolio valuation, AUM tracking, pending action alerts, and a blockchain activity feed. The sidebar exposes all asset classes and compliance tools from one interface.

slides.title: Dashboard: Identity and Verification Modules
slides.body: Scrolling down reveals the identity manager module with pending verification alerts, and the verification policy manager. Compliance is a first-class dashboard widget, not a hidden sub-menu.

slides.title: Dashboard: Portfolio Summary
slides.body: Portfolio summary cards show real-time asset valuations, 7-day performance, and the number of active assets across the platform, giving operators an instant overview of the entire managed portfolio.

slides.title: Dashboard: Asset Distribution Chart
slides.body: The dashboard donut chart visualises AUM distribution across asset types, with drill-down into individual asset categories. Useful for portfolio-level monitoring and investor reporting.

slides.title: Dashboard: Global Distribution Map
slides.body: The global map view shows the geographic spread of platform activity and investor locations. Ideal for multi-jurisdiction operations, it provides a visual overview of where assets are held and traded.

slides.title: Dashboard: Map and Statistics Detail
slides.body: A deeper view of the geographic statistics panel, showing activity volumes and investor counts by region. Supports operational oversight across multiple jurisdictions from a single screen.

slides.title: Asset Creation and Configuration
slides.subtitle: Guided wizard, pricing, compliance, permissions before deploy.

slides.title: Asset Designer: Start
slides.body: The Asset Designer is the entry point for creating any tokenised instrument. Operators select the asset class and begin configuring parameters through a structured, step-by-step wizard.

slides.title: Asset Designer: Real Estate Info
slides.body: Step 3 of the wizard captures real-world asset information such as property details, location, and legal description. These metadata fields feed directly into the deployed smart contract.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer - Step 3 - Real Estate Info.jpg

slides.title: Instrument Details
slides.body: Instrument parameters such as ISIN, maturity, coupon rate, and denomination are configured here. Every field defined at this step is encoded into the smart contract with no separate developer configuration required.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer - Step 4 - Instrument Details.jpg

slides.title: Instrument Details Form
slides.body: The detailed form view for instrument parameters allows operators to configure all financial terms. The form validates inputs before allowing progression, preventing misconfigured issuances.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer - Step 4 - Instrument Details Form.jpg

slides.title: Pricing and Valuation Setup
slides.body: Pricing model, NAV methodology, and valuation frequency are configured at this step. The platform handles market-priced instruments and NAV-based assets with equal precision.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer - Step 5 - Pricing and Valuation.jpg

slides.title: Compliance Modules Selection
slides.body: Operators choose from a library of compliance modules at design time: investor count caps, supply limits, country blocklists and allowlists, transfer approvals, and time locks. Each module is independently configurable.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer - Step 6 - Compliance Modules.jpg

slides.title: Asset Permissions Configuration
slides.body: Role-based access controls are configured per asset, defining who can mint, transfer, freeze, or manage the instrument. Permissions are enforced at the smart contract level.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer - Step 7 - Asset Permissions Config.jpg

slides.title: Asset Permissions List
slides.body: The permissions list view shows all configured roles and their associated rights for this asset. Operators can review, add, or modify role assignments before deployment.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer - Step 7 - Asset Permissions List.jpg

slides.title: Compliance: Country Allowlist
slides.body: The country allowlist compliance module restricts token holding and transfer to investors from specified jurisdictions. Supports domestic-only issuance requirements and cross-border distribution controls.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer Compliance Country Allowlist.jpg

slides.title: Compliance: Expression Builder
slides.body: The expression builder allows operators to create custom compliance logic using boolean conditions. Complex rules such as investor must be accredited AND in an approved jurisdiction can be encoded without writing code.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer Compliance Expression.jpg

slides.title: Compliance: Identity Module
slides.body: The identity compliance module links token transfers to on-chain identity verification. Only holders with verified identities matching the configured criteria can receive or hold the token.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer Compliance Identity.jpg

slides.title: Compliance Overview
slides.body: The compliance overview within the Asset Designer shows all active compliance modules configured for this asset, their parameters, and their enforcement status. Gives issuers a clear pre-deployment compliance picture.

slides.title: Permissions Panel
slides.body: The permissions panel within the Asset Designer provides a consolidated view of all role-based permissions configured for the asset, covering issuance, transfer, management, and administrative roles.

slides.title: Review and Deploy: Step 1
slides.body: Before deployment, the wizard presents a comprehensive review summary covering all configured parameters, compliance modules, and permissions. Operators verify everything is correct before committing to the blockchain.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer Review and Deploy 1.jpg

slides.title: Review and Deploy: Step 2
slides.body: The final deployment confirmation screen. Once the operator confirms, the smart contract is deployed on-chain in a single transaction. The entire configured compliance and permission framework goes live at this moment.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer Review and Deploy 2.jpg

slides.title: Supply Limit Configuration
slides.body: The supply limit compliance module sets a hard cap on the total number of tokens that can be minted for this asset. This cap is enforced at the smart contract level and cannot be exceeded.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer Supply Limit.jpg

slides.title: My Assets
slides.body: The My Assets view lists all tokenised instruments the operator has access to, with status indicators, asset type, and quick-access actions. The starting point for day-to-day asset management.

slides.title: Asset Insights
slides.body: Per-asset analytics showing price history, trading volume, holder distribution, and event timeline. Operators and investors can track the performance and activity of individual tokenised instruments.

slides.title: Portfolio Insights
slides.body: Portfolio-level analytics aggregating performance, distribution, and activity data across all managed assets. Provides the macro view needed for reporting to senior management or regulators.

slides.title: Portfolio Insights: Detail View
slides.body: A deeper cut of portfolio analytics, with drill-down into individual asset performance, holder counts, and recent corporate actions. Supports investor reporting and internal portfolio review.

slides.title: Bond Design Wizard: Step 1
slides.body: The bond-specific design wizard walks issuers through the complete process of configuring a fixed income token. Step 1 captures the high-level bond parameters and instrument classification.

slides.title: Bond Design Wizard: Step 2
slides.body: Step 2 of the bond wizard covers coupon structure configuration: rate type, payment frequency, day count convention, and first coupon date.

slides.title: Bond Design Wizard: Step 3
slides.body: Step 3 covers the bond settlement and clearing parameters, including settlement currency, clearing arrangement, and denomination structure.

slides.title: Bond Design Wizard: Step 4
slides.body: Step 4 captures investor eligibility requirements for this specific bond issuance, including accreditation status, jurisdiction restrictions, and minimum investment thresholds.

slides.title: Bond Design Wizard: Step 5
slides.body: Step 5 is the final review before bond deployment: a complete summary of all configured parameters for the issuer approval before the instrument is launched on-chain.

slides.title: Asset Classes
slides.subtitle: Bonds, equity, funds, deposits, stablecoins, metals, real estate.

slides.title: Bonds Listing
slides.body: The bonds listing view shows all active bond instruments on the platform, with status, maturity, coupon rate, and outstanding balance. Operators manage multiple bond programmes from this unified interface.

slides.title: Bond Detail: Overview
slides.body: The bond detail screen shows the full instrument profile: ISIN, maturity, coupon schedule, total supply, current holders, and outstanding balance. The starting point for any bond lifecycle management action.

slides.title: Bond Detail: Coupon and Events
slides.body: The coupon and events tab shows the complete schedule of income events, past and upcoming, including coupon payments, ex-dates, and corporate actions. All events are on-chain and auditable.

slides.title: Bond Detail: Compliance Status
slides.body: The compliance tab of the bond detail shows the current enforcement status of all configured compliance modules. Operators can see at a glance whether any compliance conditions are at risk.

slides.title: Bond Detail: Activity Log
slides.body: The activity tab logs every action taken on this bond instrument, including minting events, transfers, compliance changes, and administrative actions, providing a complete tamper-proof audit trail.

slides.title: Equity Listing
slides.body: The equity listing view shows all tokenised equity instruments on the platform, including share class, total supply, market cap, and holder count. Supports multiple concurrent equity programmes.

slides.title: Equity Detail
slides.body: The equity instrument detail view shows cap table data, voting rights configuration, dividend history, and current holder distribution. Provides the full picture for share registry management.

slides.title: Funds Listing
slides.body: The funds listing view shows all tokenised fund instruments managed on the platform, including fund type, NAV, AUM, and investor count. Supports open-ended and closed-ended fund structures.

slides.title: Fund Detail: Overview
slides.body: The fund detail screen shows NAV history, investor count, subscription and redemption status, and compliance configuration. Provides operators and fund administrators with a complete operational view.

slides.title: Fund Detail: Investors
slides.body: A deeper view into the fund investor registry: holder list, subscription dates, holding amounts, and KYC/AML status. Supports investor reporting and regulatory disclosure requirements.

slides.title: Deposits Listing
slides.body: Tokenised deposits are listed here with bank identifier, deposit type, term, interest rate, and current balance. DALP handles term deposits and demand deposits as distinct instrument types.

slides.title: Stablecoins Overview
slides.body: The stablecoins view shows all on-platform stablecoin instruments: peg currency, reserve status, total supply, and compliance configuration. Supports fiat-backed, commodity-backed, and algorithmic structures.

slides.title: Stablecoin Detail
slides.body: Stablecoin instrument detail: reserve composition, minting and redemption activity, holder distribution, and compliance module status. Full lifecycle visibility for stablecoin operators.

slides.title: Precious Metals Listing
slides.body: Tokenised precious metals programmes are listed here with vault identifier, metal type, purity, and current backing ratio. Each token represents a defined quantity of physical metal in custody.

slides.title: Precious Metal Detail
slides.body: The precious metal asset detail shows the specific metal, custody arrangement, redemption terms, and holder activity. Links the on-chain token to the physical asset record.

slides.title: Real Estate Listing
slides.body: Tokenised real estate assets listed with property type, location, valuation, and token supply. Supports fractional ownership programmes across multiple property types and geographies.

slides.title: Real Estate Assets View
slides.body: An alternative listing view for real estate assets, showing property thumbnails, valuations, and investor counts. Useful for investor-facing portals and internal portfolio management.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/10 - Real Estate/Real Estate Assets - Listing.jpg

slides.title: Real Estate: Programme Overview
slides.body: Overview screen for a real estate tokenisation programme, showing property details, token economics, and current investor activity.

slides.title: Real Estate: Asset Details
slides.body: Detailed asset profile for the Doha Business Towers tokenisation: property specifications, valuation methodology, token supply breakdown, and compliance status.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/10 - Real Estate/Real Estate - Doha Business Towers - Asset Details.jpg

slides.title: Real Estate: Data Feed Setup
slides.body: Configuring a data feed for the real estate asset, linking external property valuation or rental income data to the on-chain token. Enables automated NAV updates from trusted data sources.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/10 - Real Estate/Real Estate - Doha Business Towers - Create Data Feed.jpg

slides.title: Real Estate: Events
slides.body: The events tab logs all lifecycle events for the real estate asset, including minting, transfers, income distributions, and compliance actions. Full traceability for property token operations.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/10 - Real Estate/Real Estate - Doha Business Towers - Events.jpg

slides.title: Real Estate: Mint Tokens
slides.body: Initiating a token minting operation for the real estate asset. The operator specifies the amount and recipient, and the minting workflow enforces configured approval requirements.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/10 - Real Estate/Real Estate - Doha Business Towers - Mint Tokens.jpg

slides.title: Real Estate: Mint Confirmation
slides.body: The minting confirmation step for the real estate asset. Operators review all parameters before the tokens are minted on-chain. Once confirmed, the transaction is irreversible and fully recorded.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/10 - Real Estate/Real Estate - Doha Business Towers - Mint Tokens Confirm.jpg

slides.title: Real Estate: Verifications
slides.body: Investor verification status for the real estate token, showing KYC/AML status, accreditation, jurisdiction eligibility, and any pending verification actions required.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/10 - Real Estate/Real Estate - Doha Business Towers - Verifications.jpg

slides.title: Compliance and Identity
slides.subtitle: Policy templates, expression builder, on-chain identity.

slides.title: Compliance Policy Templates
slides.body: Reusable compliance policy templates allow operators to define compliance rules once and apply them across multiple assets. This ensures consistency and reduces configuration effort for new issuances.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Compliance Policy Templates.jpg

slides.title: Compliance Policy: Detail 1
slides.body: A detailed compliance policy view showing all configured modules, their parameters, and current enforcement status. Operators can review and modify policy settings without redeploying the asset.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Compliance Policy Detail 1.jpg

slides.title: Compliance Policy: Detail 2
slides.body: The second detail tab shows the policy usage: which assets are currently using this template, and any overrides applied at the asset level.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Compliance Policy Detail 2.jpg

slides.title: Instrument Templates: List and Create
slides.body: Instrument templates define reusable asset configurations. Standard parameter sets that issuers apply to new assets reduce time-to-market for repeat issuance programmes.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Instrument Templates - List and Create.jpg

slides.title: Instrument Template: Add Metadata Field
slides.body: Adding a custom metadata field to an instrument template. Operators extend the standard data model with asset-specific attributes such as property address for real estate or vault reference for precious metals.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Instrument Template - Add Metadata Field.jpg

slides.title: Instrument Template: Metadata Field Config
slides.body: Detailed configuration for a custom metadata field: type, validation rules, display label, and whether the field is required or optional. Supports rich data models without code changes.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Instrument Template - Metadata Field City Config.jpg

slides.title: Instrument Template: Qatar Real Estate Schema
slides.body: The full metadata schema for the Qatar Real Estate instrument template, showing all configured fields and their types. Demonstrates how DALP supports jurisdiction-specific data requirements.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Instrument Template - Qatar Real Estate - Metadata Schema.jpg

slides.title: Policy Templates: List
slides.body: All configured compliance policy templates are listed here with name, asset class, module count, and usage status. Operators manage the policy library from this view.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Policy Templates - List.jpg

slides.title: Policy Templates: Create From Scratch
slides.body: Creating a new compliance policy template from scratch. Operators name the policy, select the target asset class, and begin adding compliance modules. Templates are versioned and reusable.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Policy Templates - Create From Scratch.jpg

slides.title: Policy Template: Expression Builder
slides.body: The expression builder allows operators to define complex multi-condition compliance rules without writing code. Boolean logic conditions combine multiple compliance attributes into custom enforcement rules.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Policy Template - Expression Builder.jpg

slides.title: Policy Template: Qatar Real Estate Overview
slides.body: The overview screen for the Qatar Real Estate compliance policy template, showing all configured modules, their status, and the assets currently using this template.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Policy Template - Qatar Real Estate Token - Overview.jpg

slides.title: Policy Template: Investor Count Configuration
slides.body: Detailed configuration for the investor count compliance module within a policy template, setting the maximum number of unique holders allowed for assets using this policy.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Policy Template - Investor Count Config.jpg

slides.title: Policy Template: Investor Count Disable Dialog
slides.body: The confirmation dialog for disabling the investor count compliance module on an active policy. Operators must explicitly confirm this change, which affects all assets using the template.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Policy Template - Investor Count Disable Dialog.jpg

slides.title: Policy Template: Supply Cap Detail
slides.body: The supply cap compliance module detail, showing the configured maximum token supply, current utilisation, and the enforcement behaviour when the cap is reached.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Policy Template - Supply Cap Detail.jpg

slides.title: Policy Template: Token Supply Limit Config
slides.body: Configuration screen for the token supply limit module: setting the hard cap, configuring the overflow behaviour, and linking the limit to the asset issuance workflow.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Policy Template - Token Supply Limit Config.jpg

slides.title: On-Chain Identity
slides.body: DALP on-chain identity stores verified investor credentials on the blockchain. Identity claims including KYC status, accreditation, and jurisdiction are attached to wallet addresses and checked automatically during token transfers.

slides.title: User Management
slides.body: The user management screen shows all platform users, their roles, and their identity verification status. Operators manage access rights and compliance status for every participant from this view.

slides.title: Verification Topics
slides.body: Verification topics define the specific identity claims required for platform participation, such as KYC level, accreditation status, or professional investor certification. Each topic maps to an on-chain claim type.

slides.title: Token Operations and Settlement
slides.subtitle: Minting, transfers, freeze, XvP settlement, monitoring.

slides.title: XvP Settlement: Setup Step 1
slides.body: Initiating an XvP atomic settlement: the first party specifies the asset leg, which token, how much, and from which wallet.

slides.title: XvP Settlement: Setup Step 2
slides.body: Step 2 captures the payment leg details: settlement currency, amount, and the counterparty wallet address. Both legs are validated before the settlement flow proceeds.

slides.title: XvP Settlement: Setup Step 3
slides.body: Step 3 is the pre-execution review: both parties see the full settlement terms before committing. Either party can reject at this stage, cancelling the flow without any on-chain effect.

slides.title: XvP Settlement: Setup Step 4
slides.body: The final confirmation step for XvP settlement. Once both parties confirm, the atomic settlement executes. Both legs succeed or neither does, providing immediate on-chain finality.

slides.title: XvP Settlement: Detail Overview
slides.body: Post-settlement details for a completed XvP transaction: both legs, timestamps, wallet addresses, and on-chain transaction hashes. Provides the complete evidence record for the settlement.

slides.title: XvP Settlement: Audit Trail
slides.body: The audit tab of the XvP settlement detail shows the full event log: initiation, confirmations, validation checks, and final execution, with timestamps and participant identifiers.

slides.title: XvP API Interface
slides.body: The XvP API light view shows the programmatic interface for initiating settlements from external systems. Supports integration with trading platforms, OMS, and treasury systems via REST API.

slides.title: API Key Management
slides.body: Operators manage API credentials for platform integrations from this screen. Keys are scoped by permission level, expiry, and allowed IP ranges, following principle of least privilege for all integrations.

slides.title: Actions Panel
slides.body: The actions panel shows all pending and recent governance actions across the platform, grouped by type, status, and assigned approver. Central hub for maker-checker workflow management.

slides.title: Activity Log
slides.body: The full platform activity log, filterable by event type, asset, user, and time range. Provides compliance teams and auditors with a searchable, exportable record of all platform events.

slides.title: Activity Feed
slides.body: The real-time activity feed shows a chronological stream of platform events as they happen: identity verifications, token events, compliance changes, and settlement confirmations.

slides.title: Add-ons Marketplace
slides.body: The add-ons screen shows available platform extensions: XvP settlement, yield distribution, and other capability modules. Each add-on is independently configurable and deployable per platform instance.

slides.title: Platform Theming: Option 1
slides.body: DALP supports white-label theming: operators customise the platform visual identity including logo, colours, and typography to match their brand. Shown here: the default SettleMint theme configuration.

slides.title: Platform Theming: Option 2
slides.body: An alternative theme configuration showing how the platform appearance can be adjusted for different operator brands. Full white-labelling is supported for institutions deploying DALP under their own identity.

slides.title: Tokenise Deposit Workflow
slides.body: The deposit tokenisation workflow within settings: configuring how bank deposits are represented as tokens, including the deposit type, term, interest structure, and eligible holder criteria.

slides.title: API Monitoring: Overview
slides.body: Real-time API performance monitoring: request volume, latency, error rates, and endpoint-level statistics. Operators can identify integration issues before they affect platform users.

slides.title: API Monitoring: Request Log
slides.body: The detailed API request log shows individual API calls: endpoint, timestamp, response code, latency, and calling system. Essential for debugging integrations and demonstrating API reliability.

slides.title: Blockchain Monitoring
slides.body: On-chain monitoring shows the health and performance of the underlying blockchain network: block time, gas usage, transaction throughput, and node status. Provides operational visibility into the infrastructure layer.

slides.title: Insights: Asset Overview
slides.body: The platform-wide insights view aggregates analytics across all assets: total AUM, transaction volume, active holders, and compliance events. Supports management reporting and regulatory submissions.

slides.title: Action Queue Overview
slides.body: The actions queue showing all pending governance actions awaiting approval. Each action type is grouped and colour-coded for quick triage by operators.

slides.title: Action Approval: Step 1
slides.body: An action detail view showing the full context of a pending approval: initiator, parameters, timestamp, and required approvers. Operators review before approving or rejecting.

slides.title: Action Approval: Step 2
slides.body: Step 2 of the action approval workflow. The approver reviews the action parameters and business justification before committing their approval.

slides.title: Action Approval: Step 3
slides.body: Step 3 of the action flow. The second approver in a two-step maker-checker process reviews the action. Both approvals are required before execution.

slides.title: Action Approval: Step 4
slides.body: The completed action record showing that all required approvals were received and the action was executed. The full approval chain is preserved as an immutable audit record.

slides.title: Recent Activities Feed
slides.body: The recent activities view shows a chronological log of all platform events: token operations, compliance changes, identity verifications, and settlement confirmations. Each entry links to the full event detail.

slides.title: XvP Add-on Overview
slides.body: The XvP Exchange add-on entry screen, showing the add-on status, configuration options, and recent settlement statistics. Operators enable and configure atomic settlement from this view.

slides.title: XvP Flow: Step 1
slides.body: Initiating an XvP settlement flow: the seller specifies the asset to be delivered, the quantity, and the receiving wallet. This locks the asset leg pending counterparty confirmation.

slides.title: XvP Flow: Step 2
slides.body: The buyer confirms the payment leg: amount, currency, and the asset they expect to receive. Both legs are now specified and the system validates eligibility and balances.

slides.title: XvP Flow: Step 3
slides.body: Pre-execution validation. Both parties see the matched settlement terms. Any discrepancy at this stage prevents execution, protecting both parties from errors.

slides.title: XvP Settlement Final
slides.body: The completed XvP settlement confirmation. Both legs have been atomically executed: the asset moved to the buyer and the payment to the seller simultaneously. No partial settlement is possible.

slides.title: XvP Created Flow
slides.body: A completed settlement flow record showing the full transaction history: initiation, both party confirmations, validation, and atomic execution with on-chain transaction references.

slides.title: XvP Created Flow 2
slides.body: A second example of a completed XvP settlement flow, demonstrating the platform ability to handle concurrent settlement operations across multiple asset pairs simultaneously.

slides.title: Yield: Income Distribution Dashboard
slides.body: The yield add-on income dashboard shows scheduled distributions, pending payments, and historical yield events for all income-generating assets. Operators manage coupon payments and dividend distributions from this view.

slides.title: Forced Transfer: Step 1
slides.body: Initiating a forced transfer: the operator selects the source wallet and the token to be transferred. Forced transfers are restricted to authorised roles and require the highest level of governance approval.

slides.title: Forced Transfer: Step 2
slides.body: Step 2: specifying the destination wallet and transfer amount. The system validates that the destination is eligible to hold the token under its compliance configuration.

slides.title: Forced Transfer: Step 3
slides.body: Step 3: providing the legal or regulatory justification for the forced transfer. This documentation is recorded on-chain alongside the transfer event.

slides.title: Forced Transfer: Step 4
slides.body: Step 4: the first approver reviews all forced transfer parameters and the justification. Their approval is cryptographically signed and permanently recorded.

slides.title: Forced Transfer: Step 5
slides.body: Step 5: the second approver performs an independent review of the forced transfer. Both approvals are required as neither party can execute alone.

slides.title: Forced Transfer: Step 6
slides.body: Step 6: execution confirmation. The forced transfer has been approved and executed on-chain. The complete approval chain, justification, and transaction record are preserved as immutable evidence.

slides.title: Token Freeze: Step 0
slides.body: Initiating a token freeze: the operator selects the wallet holding and the token to freeze. Freeze actions are triggered by AML alerts, regulatory holds, or court orders.

slides.title: Token Freeze: Step 1
slides.body: Step 1 of the freeze workflow: the operator provides the reason for the freeze and any supporting reference such as a case number or regulatory instruction. This context is recorded on-chain.

slides.title: Token Freeze: Step 2
slides.body: Step 2: the freeze parameters are reviewed before submission for approval. The system shows the current holder balance and the compliance implications of the freeze.

slides.title: Token Freeze: Step 3
slides.body: Step 3: the freeze action enters the approval queue. An authorised approver reviews the request, the documented justification, and confirms the freeze.

slides.title: Token Freeze: Step 4
slides.body: Step 4: freeze execution confirmed. The token holding is now frozen, visible to the holder but non-transferable. The holder and any linked reporting systems are notified.

slides.title: Token Minting: Step 1
slides.body: Initiating token minting: the operator specifies the asset, the amount to mint, and the recipient wallet. Minting is subject to the asset supply cap and configured approval requirements.

slides.title: Token Minting: Step 2
slides.body: Step 2 of the minting workflow: reviewing the minting parameters including amount, recipient eligibility check, and remaining supply headroom, before submitting for approval.

slides.title: Token Minting: Step 3
slides.body: Step 3: the minting request enters the approval queue. The maker-checker flow ensures no single operator can mint unilaterally in environments requiring dual control.

slides.title: Token Minting: Step 4
slides.body: Step 4: minting execution confirmed. The tokens have been issued to the recipient wallet. The event is permanently recorded on-chain with the full approval chain preserved.

slides.title: Token Transfer: Step 1
slides.body: Initiating a token transfer: selecting the source wallet, destination wallet, and transfer amount. The platform immediately checks the destination eligibility under the asset compliance configuration.

slides.title: Token Transfer: Step 2
slides.body: Step 2: compliance pre-check results are displayed, including jurisdiction eligibility, identity verification status, investor count headroom, and any active restrictions on the destination wallet.

slides.title: Token Transfer: Step 3
slides.body: Step 3: if a transfer approval module is configured, the transfer enters the approval queue at this step. The request shows all compliance check results to the approver.

slides.title: Token Transfer: Step 4
slides.body: Step 4: the approver reviews the full transfer context including from wallet, to wallet, amount, compliance status, and any override justification, before approving.

slides.title: Token Transfer: Step 5
slides.body: Step 5: for assets with two-step approval, the second approver independently reviews the transfer. Both approvals are recorded cryptographically.

slides.title: Token Transfer: Step 6
slides.body: Step 6: transfer execution confirmation. The tokens have moved from source to destination. Compliance checks are re-validated at execution time to prevent race conditions.

slides.title: Token Transfer: Step 7
slides.body: Step 7: the transfer completion record, showing the on-chain transaction hash, timestamps, wallet addresses, and full approval chain. The definitive audit record for this transfer.

slides.title: Token Unfreeze: Step 0
slides.body: Initiating an unfreeze: the operator selects the frozen wallet and token. Unfreeze can only be initiated by authorised roles and requires documented justification matching the governance standard of the original freeze.

slides.title: Token Unfreeze: Step 1
slides.body: Step 1: the operator provides the reason for unfreezing and any supporting reference such as regulatory clearance or court order. Recorded on-chain alongside the freeze record.

slides.title: Token Unfreeze: Step 2
slides.body: Step 2: the unfreeze parameters are reviewed before submission. The system shows the freeze history and the original freeze justification for context.

slides.title: Token Unfreeze: Step 3
slides.body: Step 3: the unfreeze request enters the approval queue. An authorised approver reviews the request against the original freeze context and the provided clearance justification.

slides.title: Token Unfreeze: Step 4
slides.body: Step 4: unfreeze execution confirmed. The token holding is now active again. The complete freeze and unfreeze lifecycle with all approvals and justifications is preserved as an immutable audit trail.

slides.title: Bond Tokenisation: Start
slides.body: The opening screen of the bond tokenisation wizard. Operators are presented with a structured flow overview before beginning configuration, ensuring all required information is prepared in advance.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/01 Step Bond Tokenisation.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Instrument Selection
slides.body: Step 2: selecting the instrument type. The wizard presents the available fixed income sub-types including standard bond, green bond, sukuk, and convertible note, each with a pre-configured parameter template.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/02 Step Bond Choosing Instrument .jpg

slides.title: Bond: Information Entry
slides.body: Step 3: entering the bond identifying information including name, ISIN, issuer name, and governing law. This data forms the bond digital identity on the platform.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/03 Step Bond Information.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Instrument Details
slides.body: Step 4: configuring the core financial parameters including face value, denomination, maturity date, coupon rate, coupon frequency, and day count convention.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/04 Step Instrument Details .jpg

slides.title: Bond: Pricing and Valuation
slides.body: Step 5: setting the pricing model and NAV methodology for the bond. Operators configure the pricing source, valuation frequency, and the initial issuance price.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/05 Step Bond pricing and valuation .jpg

slides.title: Bond: Compliance Modules
slides.body: Step 6: selecting compliance modules for this bond. Each module adds an enforceable constraint to the token. Operators choose exactly the restrictions required for this specific issuance.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/06 Step Complaince Modules.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Supply Cap
slides.body: Step 7: configuring the supply cap, which is the maximum number of bond tokens that can be minted. This represents the total issuance size and is enforced as a hard constraint on the smart contract.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/07 Step Supply Cap .jpg

slides.title: Bond: Collateral Requirement
slides.body: Step 8: defining collateral requirements for the bond issuance. Operators specify the collateral type, minimum ratio, and monitoring parameters for collateral-backed instruments.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/08 Step Colleteral Requirement.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Time Lock
slides.body: Step 9: configuring time-based restrictions including lock-up periods, transfer embargo windows, or holding period requirements. Time locks are enforced automatically at the smart contract level.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/09 Step Time Lock .jpg

slides.title: Bond: Block Addresses
slides.body: Step 10: entering wallet addresses that should be blocked from holding or transacting this bond. Used for sanctions enforcement and known-bad-actor exclusions.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/10 Step Block Addresses.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Transfer Approval
slides.body: Step 11: configuring transfer approval requirements, including whether all secondary transfers require explicit approval and how many approvers are needed. Supports T+2 settlement models with manual oversight.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/11 Step Transfer Approval.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Country Blocklist
slides.body: Step 12: selecting countries whose residents are blocked from holding this bond. Supports sanctions compliance and jurisdiction-restricted distribution for cross-border issuances.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/12 Step Country Blocklist.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Identity Blocklist
slides.body: Step 13: blocking specific on-chain identity credentials from eligibility. Complements the country blocklist with individual-level exclusions based on verified identity attributes.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/13 Step Identity Blocklist.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Investor Count Cap
slides.body: Step 14: setting the maximum number of unique investors allowed to hold this bond. Enforces private placement limits such as the 500-investor rule for Reg D or equivalent exemptions.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/14 Step Investor count.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Identity Allowlist
slides.body: Step 15: defining the identity allowlist. Only holders with specific verified credentials can receive this bond. Supports accredited investor requirements and professional investor restrictions.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/15 Step Identity allowlist.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Token Supply Limit
slides.body: Step 16: configuring the hard token supply limit, a secondary cap that prevents the total token count from exceeding the configured maximum regardless of other issuance actions.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/16 Step Token Supply Limit.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Smart Identity Verification
slides.body: Step 17: configuring smart identity verification, which performs automatic eligibility checks against the on-chain identity registry at the time of every transfer. No manual KYC check required per transfer.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/17 Step Smart Identity Verification.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Country Allowlist
slides.body: Step 18: specifying allowed countries. Only investors from listed jurisdictions can hold or receive this bond. Works in conjunction with the country blocklist for precise jurisdiction control.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/18 Step Country allowlist.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Asset Permissions
slides.body: Step 19: final permissions configuration, assigning specific platform roles to the minting, transfer, freeze, and management functions of this bond. Locks down who can perform each action.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/19 Step Asset Permissions.jpg

slides.title: Bond: Review and Deploy
slides.body: Step 20: the final review screen. All 19 configuration steps are summarised for operator sign-off. Once confirmed, the bond smart contract is deployed on-chain, fully configured and live.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/01. Fixed Income/Bond Tokenisation/20 Step Review and Deploy.jpg

slides.title: Equity Tokenisation: Start
slides.body: Opening the equity tokenisation wizard. The wizard supports tokenising ordinary shares, preference shares, and convertible instruments through the same structured configuration flow.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/02. Flexible Income/Equity/01 Step.jpg

slides.title: Equity: Step 2
slides.body: Step 2: configuring the equity instrument identifying details including company name, share class, ISIN, and governing law. These fields define the token legal identity.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/02. Flexible Income/Equity/02 Step.jpg

slides.title: Equity: Step 3
slides.body: Step 3: entering equity-specific parameters including authorised share count, voting rights configuration, and dividend policy. These settings encode the share economic and governance rights.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/02. Flexible Income/Equity/03 Step .jpg

slides.title: Equity: Step 4
slides.body: Step 4: configuring distribution restrictions including transfer restrictions, lock-up periods, and pre-emption rights. Protects the cap table integrity for controlled share issuances.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/02. Flexible Income/Equity/04 Step.jpg

slides.title: Equity: Pricing Configuration
slides.body: Step 5: configuring the equity pricing model including initial issuance price, valuation methodology, and reference data source. Supports mark-to-market and book-value pricing models.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/02. Flexible Income/Equity/05 Step Tokenised Equity Pricing .jpg

slides.title: Equity: Compliance Modules
slides.body: Step 6: selecting compliance modules for the equity token. Typical modules include accredited investor restrictions, transfer approval, and investor count caps for private placements.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/new-2026-03-24/Asset Management/1. Asset Designer/02. Flexible Income/Equity/06 Step Tokenised Equtiy Complaince Modules.jpg
